steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content: Both terms in $v^4-480v^3$ contain $v^3$. Factor that out first instead of trying to multiply the binomial choices. This keeps the degree and the common power of $v$ visible.

steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content: After taking out $v^3$, the remaining factor is $v-480$. Expanding the factored form would return the original two terms, so the expressions are equivalent. $$v^4-480v^3=v^3(v-480)$$ The answer is C.
